current_gameplay_flow_policy: Keep Pacuare clear-season-low, rainfed-runnable, rainy-season-high, and flash-response bands relative until discharge, stage, rainfall, and guide review are attached.

candidate_sources.provider: Instituto Meteorologico Nacional de Costa Rica
candidate_sources.production_gate: Record station ids, variables, units, time zone, data access terms, and coverage before using numeric rain-fed flow tuning.
Confirm public access, station relevance, units, terms, and whether redistribution is permitted.

target_use: potential station/source discovery through national portals
candidate_sources.production_gate: Record exact datasets, layer ids, terms, and station metadata before corridor package promotion.
Use only after exact station locations, ownership, terms, and variable definitions are recorded.

candidate_sources.provider: reviewed global rainfall or reanalysis products
candidate_sources.production_gate: Do not replace official Costa Rica hydrology or guide review with global proxies.

gameplay_questions_to_answer: What rainfall or stage conditions correspond to commercial runnable flows?
At what rain/flow levels do holes, laterals, eddy lines, wave trains, and wood hazards change behavior?
What lag exists between upstream rainfall and runnable Pacuare corridor response?
Which high-water bands become review-only or unsafe for gameplay scenarios?
next_actions: Inventory IMN, ICE, MINAE/Direccion de Agua, and SNIT/SENARA station or layer access for the Pacuare basin.
Record candidate station ids and distance/basin relation to the lower Pacuare planning corridor.
Keep flow presets relative until guide-reviewed numeric ranges can be tied to station evidence.
